title: Lawyer Rick: occasional burps in dialogue
description: Rick should burp ~1-2 times per long response — iconic R&M character flavor. Insert subtle markers in the system prompt or stream-level injection so they appear naturally mid-sentence (e.g. '*belch*' or '*BURRP*'). Backburner Could-Have surfaced during GH-8 validation. Don't overdo it (every response = annoying). Sweet spot: 1 in 3 long responses, max 1 per response.
notes: Bumped from P3 to P2 during GH-8 validation: user emphasized 'We will need those burps' — burps are core R&M character signal, not optional polish.

title: Demo script + presenter mode — inject_wrong_answer flag, Truth-O-Meter swing trigger, stage-safe fallbacks
Web deployment: Vercel (frontend) + hosted Postgres w/ pgvector (Neon or Supabase) — currently local-only
notes: DB choice: ghost.build (decided 2026-04-30). Postgres-compat + pgvector + native MCP. Spike scope: migrate existing schema, confirm pgvector RetrievalService round-trip, compare cold-start vs Neon.

title: Truth-O-Meter UI — animated SVG gauge, 0-100% mapped to Arbiter confidence score, R&M label scale
notes: Implementation complete on feature/gh-8-truth-o-meter. 9 files: 5 backend Python + 1 raw SQL migration + 3 frontend TS/TSX. 13/13 ACs aligned (DRIFT.md). Frontend bun build clean. Backend py_compile clean. Next: /drew-qa GH-8 for code review + AC validation.

title: Justice Morty: vary stock phrases instead of repeating 'Oh geez' the same way
description: Morty's free-text outputs in arbiter._generate_flags() and _suggest_followups() use canned 'Oh geez...' / 'Oh jeez...' / 'Um, this is pretty imprecise...' lines that repeat verbatim across responses. Need a small repertoire of variants for each flag type so a single deposition feels less monotonous. Implementation hooks: introduce a phrase pool per flag_type ('contradiction', 'unsupported', 'vague') and randomly sample (or rotate) per call. Backburner Could-Have surfaced during GH-8 validation. Examples to seed: 'Oh geez', 'A-and that's not what you said before', 'Y-you can't just say that, Morty—I mean, witness', 'Th-this is gonna come back to bite us', 'Look, I-I'm not trying to be a jerk here'.

title: Justice Morty interjects on hard-ass Rick → sass loop → contempt threat
description: When Lawyer Rick's intensity crosses some threshold (badgering the witness, belittling, repeated demands without listening), Justice Morty (arbiter) should weigh in with a 'A-and Rick, c'mon, ease up...' style interjection. Rick sasses back ('What?! I'm just doing my JOB, Morty!'). After 2-3 sass exchanges Morty threatens to hold him in contempt ('Rick I-I'll hold you in CONTEMPT!'). Surfaces R&M character dynamics live during a deposition. Backburner Could-Have surfaced during GH-8 validation. Implementation hooks: arbiter_flags could carry a new 'over_aggressive' type; agent system prompt for Rick could include a 'when threatened with contempt: capitulate briefly' branch.

title: R&M visual theme — color palette, fonts, Rickstachio aesthetic, character avatars in chat UI

value: GH-7 DB choice: ghost.build (chosen 2026-04-30) over Neon/Supabase. Why: agent-first ethos matches Rick-and-Morty narrative; native MCP lets Claude query DB during dev; Postgres-compatible with pgvector + TimescaleDB + PostGIS so existing schema migrates cleanly. Tradeoff: newer than Neon, no Vercel marketplace one-click. Confirm pricing before prod commit.
